verdict: N=11 and N=17 do NOT admit a 107-style canonical-invariant identification. (T1) Neither is a power sum of the H_3 exponent or degree multisets (those are [15, 18, 107, 140, 855, 1224]: 15,107 and 18,140), whereas 107 IS sum m_i^2 and 18 IS sum d_i. (T2) 107 is hit by very few canonical formulas (2), while 11 and 17 are each hit by many (10, 4) -- small integers are not distinctive. (T3) The operative 6k-1 ladder is real (11=h+1, 17=sum d_i -1, spacing d_2=6) but its generator choice, -1 offset, and generation indexing are un-derived handles. So N=11,17 cannot be promoted via a canonical invariant the way 107 was; the Tier-3 status stands, and the residual is the non-perturbative lepton-eigenvalue extraction (O.5).
